display.purpose: Hero identity and major section headings.

shadows.purpose: Base depth for dark-mode panel surfaces.

shadows.purpose: Interactive glow response for light-mode scanner effects.

shadows.purpose: Interactive glow response for dark-mode scanner effects.

components.name: Interactive Utility Button
components.description: Base utility control used by theme and menu toggles, with scanner overlay and focus ring.
components.html: <button class="ds-control" aria-label="Toggle"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" width="20" height="20" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M17.293 13.293A8 8 0 016.707 2.707a8.001 8.001 0 1010.586 10.586z" fill="currentColor"/></svg></button>

components.name: Navigation Signal Link
components.description: Primary nav item with icon pairing and animated signal underline.

components.name: Signal Tag Chip
components.description: Rounded metadata chip for tags with amber light-mode and teal dark-mode semantics.

components.name: Pagination Button
components.description: Bordered pagination unit with scanner hover and accent-backed active state.

components.name: Surface Panel
components.description: Signature translucent container with large radius, border rail, blur, and ambient shadow.

narrative.overview: This system now reads as neon-industrial rather than minimal cyber: dual accent signals (amber + teal), layered atmospheric backgrounds, and scanner-like interaction effects shape the visual voice. It preserves technical readability through strong contrast and stable typography while allowing controlled cinematic expression.
keyCharacteristics: Dual-accent signal language (amber and teal) used as semantic emphasis rails.
Atmosphere layers framing content without reducing legibility.
Scanner and pulse interaction motifs on controls and navigation.
Rounded utility controls and stronger panel geometry for grouping.
Code and table theming integrated into the same cybernetic palette.

rules.name: The Dual-Signal Rule
rules.body: Amber and teal must appear as paired signals, never as competing decorative floods.

rules.name: The Read-First Rule
rules.body: Body text remains high-contrast against neutral fields; atmosphere layers never degrade readability.

rules.name: The Dual-Voice Rule
rules.body: Headings carry identity; body copy carries trust. Do not swap these duties.

rules.name: The Controlled-Glow Rule
rules.body: Glow is reserved for interaction and framing moments; persistent ambient haze must stay subtle.

dos: Do preserve the existing cyberpunk identity and visual energy while keeping body copy contrast at readable levels.
Do use amber and teal as a coordinated dual-signal pair.
Do keep scanner/pulse motion tied to interaction states, with reduced-motion parity.
Do maintain dark/light mode parity so hierarchy and clarity remain consistent in either theme.
donts: Don't drift into generic SaaS dashboard aesthetics.
Don't replace the current visual language with a neutral redesign that erases the existing cyberpunk identity and visual energy.
Don't introduce side-stripe accent borders or hero-metric template scaffolding.
